02 What Is An Operating System - YouTube.mp4
All this talk about operating systems begs the question, what is an operating system anyway? Well, when I Googled that very same question, I discovered that an operating system is a set of algorithms and a common language that enables the different components to communicate with one another in support of the desired outputs of a machine. Now, this definition applies to a computer operating system, but if you think about it, in many respects, your business is a lot like a modern computer. I mean, the set of algorithms. Well, those are just your systems, processes, and standard operating procedures. When we think about a common language, that's stuff like your meeting rhythms, scorecards, and just the general communication architecture of your company and desired outputs. Well, that's your company's goals and objectives.
The problem is when you're running on a U OS, a U operating system, the set of algorithms, well, those are stuck in your head. Common language is just kind of a bad version of the telephone game, and the desired outputs are usually something along the lines of, 'just don't run out of money, right? Just don't die.' And this leaves everyone in the company to pursue their own version of what they believe is best for the business, which may or may not be right or wrong. This is why it's critical that you build and install a scalable operating system that one— documents the set of algorithms that makes your business run. defines your company's communication architecture, and clarifies the company's desired outputs so your people and systems are all aligned to a common goal. So, that is our objective. Now, let's talk about how we're going to make it happen.
